reports.text:
The Refining Liquidity Trap

OECD commercial diesel and gasoline inventories will collapse to a critical 20 days of demand by August and October if the Strait remains persistently shut without aggressive market solvers.  

Retail bulls are blind to the alpha trap in refining configuration. A 1pp rise in US diesel yield forcefully reduces finished gasoline yield by 0.8pp, turning product availability into a violent zero-sum game.  

This regime shift triggers a brutal bottleneck. While a $10 increase in the 3-2-1 refining margin historically boosts global utilization by 1.3pp, physical capacity limits and rigid price caps mean refiners cannot pass on soaring crude costs.  

Bottom Line:
Expect an aggressive drawdown in consumer discretionary margins. Avoiding an outright supply crunch requires a punishing 3% to 4% demand destruction driven entirely by surging pump prices.

• 📑 Company to undertake requisite regulatory filings, including filing certified order with Registrar of Companies

• 📢 Company said stock exchanges will be informed regarding all material developments including effective date of scheme

🟢 Impact: Positive (NCLT approval advances restructuring process and could improve operational alignment and business consolidation)

🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 19 May 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

VASCON ENGINEERS: Bags ₹131.58 Cr work order from Reliance Industries for construction of G+12 buildings at Jamnagar project, to be completed in 19 months.

TRIL: Secures ₹175 Cr transformer order from Bhanuvirat Infra Projects for 400/220 KV GSS Kumher and Amber transmission projects.

BRAHMAPUTRA INFRASTRUCTURE JV: Wins ₹114.24 Cr railway contract from North Central Railway for construction of Road Over Bridges under Gati Shakti initiative.

DESCO INFRATECH: Emerges L1 bidder for Gujarat Gas integrated PE pipeline laying and PNG connection project in Surat & Hazira GA.

NIBE: Successfully test fires SURYASTRA rocket launcher system with 150 km and 300 km precision strike capability validated in trials.

AIRFLOW RAIL TECHNOLOGY: Secures ₹2.68 Cr order from Indian Railways (ICF Chennai) for supply and installation of interior paneling systems.

DHARA RAIL PROJECTS: Bags ₹3.69 Cr railway maintenance order from Ministry of Railways for LHB coach electrical refurbishment work.

REMSONS INDUSTRIES: Subsidiary wins ₹30 Cr global OEM nomination for hood rod supply contract for commercial vehicle platform.

---

📍 ACQUISITION

ITC: Raises stake in Mother Sparsh Baby Care to 49.32% for ~₹30 Cr, strengthening presence in premium baby care segment.

JSW ENERGY: Signs agreement to increase stake in Toshiba JSW Power Systems to 20.7% for ₹150 Cr to secure turbine supply chain.

---

📍 COLLABORATION

NIIT LEARNING SYSTEMS: Partners with Visier for AI-led workforce transformation and people analytics solutions in global markets.

AU SMALL FINANCE BANK: Partners with Intellect to adopt “Purple Fabric” AI platform for enterprise-wide AI banking transformation.

ADVait ENERGY: Signs MoU with Norway’s TECO Fuel Cell for hydrogen fuel cell technology development under green energy mission.

---

📍 LEADERSHIP CHANGES

CYIENT DLM: Appoints Ramakanth Alapati as President & Chief Strategy and Growth Officer to drive global expansion and AI-led manufacturing strategy.

---

📍 NEW PROJECTS

SAHAJ SOLAR: Forms JV for 750 MW solar panel manufacturing facility in UAE with Clarion Investment on 50:50 basis.

BHARAT FORGE: To set up India’s first private marine gas turbine facility in Visakhapatnam under defence manufacturing corridor.

EPACK DURABLE: Receives Rajasthan RIPS-2024 incentives for Bhiwadi plant including 10-year turnover-linked incentive support.

---

📍 FUNDRAISING

DHANUKA AGRITECH: Approves ₹70 Cr share buyback at ₹1,400 per share via tender offer route.

---

📍 CREDIT RATINGS

APOLLO HOSPITALS: ICRA reaffirms AAA/Stable long-term and A1+ short-term ratings for ₹3,000 Cr bank facilities.

---

📍 FII STAKE CHANGES / BOUGHT-SOLD

ICICI PRUDENTIAL LIFE: Standard Chartered likely to exit bancassurance partnership; Bharti Life may replace as partner.

---

📍 Q2 / BUSINESS UPDATE

GENUS POWER: Reports strong Q4 with 62.7% YoY revenue growth and ₹25,173 Cr order book; signals FY27 as major execution year.

MANBA FINANCE: GNPA/NNPA improve to 3.33%/2.46%; credit losses remain below 1% with strong provisioning discipline.

SHANKARA BUILDPRO: FY26 revenue up 30% YoY and PAT up 64%; targets ~1.2 mn tonnes steel volume in FY27.

HINDALCO (NOVELIS): Q4 hit by Oswego fire losses of $630 Mn; reports net loss of $84 Mn amid shipment decline.

EUREKA FORBES: Q4 revenue grows 11.6% YoY with improved margins and strong service business momentum.

ZYDUS LIFESCIENCES: Strong FY26 driven by biosimilars, consumer wellness +61% YoY and global expansion.

ORKLA: Spices business grows 6.1% in Q4; domestic growth strong excluding Kerala restructuring impact.

PARADEEP PHOSPHATES: NCLAT rejects entry tax exemption appeal in Odisha case; plans Supreme Court challenge.

NITCO: Targets ₹1,000 Cr revenue by FY29 with ~30% CAGR driven by premiumisation and expansion.

KWALITY PHARMACEUTICALS: Targets ₹650+ Cr revenue by FY27 with strong biologics and regulated market expansion.

ADITYA VISION: Expands retail footprint to 209 stores with new outlets in Agra and Patna.

CAMS: Reports strong rise in women mutual fund participation with ₹11.3 lakh crore AUM contribution.

BALRAMPUR CHINI: Launches women-focused 3D printing skill development program under sustainability initiative.

EMROCK: Approves Bio-CNG project with 1,600 TPA capacity expansion into renewable energy.

ZIM LABS (EU): Gains Italy approval for Dabigatran capsules boosting global pharma reach.

GODAWARI POWER: Commissions 25 MW captive solar plant to reduce energy cost and emissions.

---

📍 OTHERS

HIMADRI SPECIALITY: Increases investment in Australian battery tech firm Sicona via convertible notes.

GODFREY PHILLIPS: Receives ₹100 Cr insurance payout after tobacco facility fire incident.

SHELTER PHARMA: Reports continuous positive customer feedback across product portfolio.

KWALITY PHARMA / HINDALCO / ZYDUS: Mixed corporate updates reflecting sector-specific growth and disruptions.

BALRAMPUR FOUNDATION: Launches women skilling initiative in bioplastic 3D printing ecosystem.

GODAWARI POWER & ISPAT LTD COMMISSIONS 25 MW CAPTIVE SOLAR PLANT ☀️⚙️

- Company commissioned and synchronized 25 MWp captive solar power plant with grid on May 19, 2026  

- Plant located at:
  - Chawardhal, Rajnandgaon district, Chhattisgarh  

- Solar power to be used for captive consumption at:
  - Ari Dongri iron ore mine  
  - Boria Tibu iron ore mine  

- Project aimed at:
  - Reducing dependence on high-cost DISCOM power  
  - Improving energy efficiency  
  - Supporting green energy initiatives  

- Plant expected to help:
  - Lower operating power costs  
  - Reduce carbon footprint  

Final Takeaway
- Captive solar commissioning strengthens Godawari Power’s backward integration and energy cost optimisation strategy while supporting sustainability initiatives across mining operations  

Impact
- Positive

INDIA’S LITHIUM IMPORT BILL IS EXPLODING — BUT THE REAL STORY IS CELLS, NOT LITHIUM 🔋📈

- Lithium imports have risen nearly 10x:
  - ₹3,532 Cr in FY18  
  - ₹37,624 Cr in Apr–Feb FY26 alone  

- FY26 imports already 48% higher than full FY25  

- India is not importing raw lithium at scale  
- India is importing finished lithium-ion cells, largely from China  

- Every:
  - EV scooter  
  - BESS project  
  - Grid storage tender  
  - Energy transition rollout  

still depends heavily on imported cells  

Government Push
- India launched major policy support:
  - ₹18,100 Cr ACC PLI Scheme  
  - ₹34,300 Cr National Critical Mineral Mission  
  - ₹1,500 Cr Recycling Incentive Scheme  

Current Reality Check
- Only Ola Electric has commercially produced cells so far  
- Around 1.5 GWh production achieved vs 20 GWh allocation target  

- Q3FY26 Ola cell business:
  - 72,418 cells produced  
  - ₹9 Cr revenue  
  - ₹39 Cr EBITDA loss  

- Reliance New Energy and Rajesh Exports yet to scale meaningfully  
- EV penetration already crossed 8.5%  
- India targeting 30% EV penetration by 2030  

Upstream Challenge
- 5.9 million tonne lithium reserve in Reasi, J&K:
  - Auction attempted twice  
  - Both failed  
  - No qualified bidders  

- Government now conducting re-exploration  

Key Investment Theme
- Real question is not:
  - “Will India import less lithium?”  

- Real question is:
  - “Which companies survive and scale when domestic ecosystem finally matures?”  

---

CELL & BATTERY PACK PLAYERS 🔋

- Exide Industries
  - 6 GWh Bengaluru plant  
  - SVOLT technology partnership  
  - Commercial production targeted by FY26-end  

- Amara Raja Energy & Mobility
  - 16 GWh Telangana gigafactory  
  - ₹9,500 Cr capex  
  - NMC chemistry production targeted by FY27-end  

- Waaree Energies
  - 20 GWh integrated cell + BESS facility approved  
  - ₹8,175 Cr Andhra Pradesh project  

- JSW Energy
  - 10 GWh target by 2026  
  - Scaling to 50 GWh by 2030  
  - Exploring lithium refining downstream  

- Reliance Industries
  - 10 GWh PLI allocation  
  - Jamnagar battery ecosystem ramp-up underway  

- Ola Electric
  - Only listed company currently manufacturing cells in India  
  - Key monitorable:
    - Bharat Cell economics and scaling  

---

CATHODE, ANODE & ELECTROLYTE PLAYS ⚗️

- Himadri Speciality Chemical
  - India’s first commercial anode plant commissioned  
  - ₹1,125 Cr LFP cathode project  
  - Long-term target:
    - 2,00,000 MTPA LFP capacity  

- Graphite India
  - ₹4,330 Cr committed toward synthetic graphite anode materials  

- HEG (via TACC)
  - Large anode material plans underway  

- Gujarat Fluorochemicals
  - LiPF6 electrolyte salt plant commissioned  
  - ~$1 Bn EV materials investment roadmap  

- Neogen Chemicals
  - 30,000 MT electrolyte facility  
  - Mitsubishi Chemical technology partnership  

- Ami Organics
  - Electrolyte salts development outside China  
  - ₹300 Cr Gujarat facility  

- Tata Chemicals
  - Cathode materials  
  - Cell ecosystem  
  - Recycling partnerships with ISRO & CSIR-CECRI  

---

BATTERY RECYCLING & CIRCULAR ECONOMY ♻️

- India expected to generate:
  - 128 GWh end-of-life batteries by 2030  

- Recycling economics attractive:
  - Lithium recovery:
    - 18–30 kg per tonne from recycling  
    - Only 2–7 kg from fresh ore  

- Gravita India
  - Expanding from lead recycling into lithium-ion  

- Exide Industries
  - Greenfield recycling facility commissioned  

- Amara Raja
  - Integrated recycling strategy underway  

- Tata Chemicals
  - Commercial lithium-ion recycling since 2019  
  - 99%+ purity claims  
  - Scaling toward 500 tonnes annually  

Final Takeaway
- India’s lithium story is increasingly shifting from EV sales toward localisation of the entire battery value chain. The real strategic battleground sits in cells, cathodes, anodes, electrolytes and recycling rather than raw lithium mining alone. Demand is already visible, but domestic supply ecosystems are still in early stages.

The companies that successfully scale technology, manufacturing and integration over FY27–FY30 could potentially become critical beneficiaries of India’s long-term energy transition and storage buildout  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term Structural Theme

Invicta Diagnostic Limited Reports FY26 Total Income of ₹33.04 Cr with EBITDA Margins Above 30%

Invicta Diagnostic Limited, one of the leading diagnostic service providers in Maharashtra’s healthcare services sector, has announced its Audited Financial Results for H2 FY26.

Consolidated Key Financial Highlights H2 FY26

• Total Income of ₹ 15.96 Cr
• EBITDA of ₹ 3.55 Cr
• EBITDA Margin of 22.24%
• Net Profit of ₹ 0.79 Cr
• Net Profit Margin of 4.94%
• EPS of ₹ 0.79

Consolidated Key Financial Highlights FY26

• Total Income increased to ₹ 33.04 Cr from ₹ 30.18 Cr annually
• EBITDA increased to ₹ 10.45 Cr from ₹ 9.29 Cr annually
• EBITDA Margins increased to maintained at 31.63% from 30.78%
• Net Profit of ₹ 4.87 Cr
• Net Profit Margin of 14.74%
• EPS of ₹ 4.90

During FY26, the Company invested ₹12.96 Cr towards fixed assets and network expansion. Depreciation increased by 31% to ₹3.25 Cr from ₹2.49 Cr due to the higher asset base. As newly added centres mature and scale up, the Company expects stronger revenue contribution and improved operating leverage going forward.

Operational Highlights:

• New Centre with MRI & CT coming up in Pune
• Commenced commercial operations at the Dadar Centre from 30 January 2026, strengthening its Maharashtra presence near Tata Memorial Hospital
• Strategic entry in Nashik with a 51% acquisition of stake in Vinchurkar Diagnostics Private Limited

Currently, the stock is trading at ₹80.80, with a Mcap of ₹102.00 Cr (As on 19th May 2026)

BALRAMPUR CHINI FOUNDATION LAUNCHES WOMEN-FOCUSED 3D PRINTING SKILL PROGRAM 🧑‍🏭🖨️

• 🎓 Balrampur Foundation launched “Building Skills. Transforming Futures – Balrampur Bioyug Bioplastic 3D Printing Project” in collaboration with Balrampur Bioyug & ITI Mohammadi

• 👩‍💻 Initiative focused on empowering women ITI students with advanced manufacturing & entrepreneurship skills through 3D printing technology

• 🏭 Advanced infrastructure including 3D printers, laptops, dryers, enclosures & Bioyug PLA materials installed at ITI Mohammadi

• 🌱 BCML highlighted that it is setting up India’s first PLA plant with 80,000 TPA capacity

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens ESG and sustainability positioning while supporting future PLA ecosystem and women-focused skilling initiatives)

• 💨 Proposed annual Bio-CNG production capacity of 1,600 Tons

• 🌱 Project will also produce 8,652.8 Tons of Fermented Organic Manure (FOM)

• 💧 Liquid Fermented Organic Manure (LFOM) production capacity planned at 14,732.8 Tons annually

• 💰 Total estimated project cost pegged at ₹30–33 Crore

• 🏦 Funding to be arranged through fresh capital infusion and bank/financial institution assistance

• ⏳ Project expected to be completed on or before June 30, 2027, subject to approvals

• 🌍 Initiative marks company’s entry into Bio-CNG & renewable energy segment to explore long-term growth opportunities

🟢 Impact: Positive (Expansion into renewable energy and Bio-CNG segment can diversify revenue streams and strengthen long-term growth prospects)

GNFC: INDIA’S HIDDEN TECHNICAL-GRADE UREA MONOPOLY

Industry Position
- GNFC remains sole major domestic TGU producer.
- TGU used for Adblue and DEF manufacturing.
- DEF mandatory for BS6 diesel vehicles.

Why DEF Matters
- DEF required for SCR emission systems.
- Vehicles may stop without DEF usage.
- Used across trucks, buses and SUVs.

Demand-Supply Gap
- India needs 6 lakh tonnes annually.
- Domestic capacity near 1.5 lakh tonnes.
- Majority demand historically met through imports.

Import Disruption Impact
- Imports mainly sourced from Dubai and Egypt.
- West Asia disruptions impacted shipping routes.
- Domestic supply importance rising sharply.

Government & Industry Push
- Government discussing domestic output expansion.
- SIAM reportedly pushing faster TGU availability.
- Expansion may support automotive supply chains.

Why GNFC Matters
- Practical commercial capacity rests with GNFC.
- GSFC capacity available only temporarily.
- New capacity may strengthen market dominance.

Pricing & Margin Opportunity
- TGU commands premium over fertiliser-grade urea.
- Industrial pricing remains non-subsidised.
- Expansion may directly improve margins.

Broader Business Portfolio
- Also manufactures TDI and methanol products.
- Portfolio includes acetic and formic acid.
- Chemical business remains commodity-cycle linked.

Key Risks
- Government intervention risk remains possible.
- Fertiliser supply balancing remains sensitive.
- Commodity chemical pricing remains cyclical.

Final Takeaway
- GNFC may be emerging as India’s strategic technical-grade urea supplier amid DEF demand growth and import disruptions.

MTF books are growing across brokers despite the broader markets going nowhere. This isn't like the Korean markets, for example, where the markets are up 150% in the last year alone, and people are borrowing to ride that rally. Our situation is different.

The big risk with MTF is the risk of the stock becoming illiquid in case there's a sharp market fall. If a stock moves more than the margin provided (say 20%), the bad debit is on the broker. The odds of recovering a loss from a customer aren't that great.

The risk shoots up when the collateral is stocks. A customer pledges Stock A, gets 80% margin on it, and uses that to take further positions worth 400% in the same stock. If that stock is a mid or small-cap stock, circuits kick in, and there's simply no exit if markets turn around. Nearly 50% of the entire industry MTF book is non-F&O stocks.

While we still don’t allow collateral margin for buying MTF, competitive pressure would mean we will have to. There's significant risk on the customer, but also on the broker. While our MTF book has grown meaningfully over the last 16 months, it's still only about 25% of our networth. For some brokers, this number could be closer to 500% which is the maximum regulator allows.

If markets crash, brokers could end up holding losses from MTF positions they can't exit and that puts the entire ecosystem at risk.

MTF seems like easy money for the brokers. But the Risk Management team at brokers has to make sure that on one bad day, you don't give it all back. 😬

Captain Polyplast Limited Secures ₹11.8 Cr Order from MSEDCL for 500 Solar Pumps

Captain Polyplast Limited (CPL) has secured a significant new order from Maharashtra State Electricity Distribution Company Limited (MSEDCL) for the supply of 500 solar pumps, with a total order value of ₹11.8 Cr (inclusive of GST) under the PM-KUSUM Scheme.

Order Highlights:
* Order received from MSEDCL for 500 Solar Pumps
* Total Order Value: ₹11.8 Cr (inclusive of GST)
* Aligned with Government of India's PM-KUSUM Scheme
* Execution through CPL's existing manufacturing and operational infrastructure

Order at a Glance:
This order reinforces Captain Polyplast Limited's growing capabilities in delivering large-scale solar pumping solutions to government and utility clients - enhancing revenue visibility and supporting steady growth in the solar pump segment, contributing to a more diversified business mix.

This milestone further cements Captain Polyplast Limited's position as a preferred partner for state-level agencies in the solar pumping space - reinforcing its commitment to delivering quality renewable energy solutions across India.

Patil Automation Limited | Execution Firing, Capacity Unlock Driving Next Leg of Growth
_H2 FY26 Earnings Concall Highlights_

Operational Highlights
* New Pune facility scaling well, adding ~₹50 Cr incremental revenue, while legacy plant remained fully utilized.
* Subsidiary integration gaining traction with Pentaco (~₹18 Cr) and MII Robotics (₹2.3 Cr) operating under a unified setup.
* Strong visibility with ~₹118 Cr order book, split ~60% automotive and ~40% non-auto (defence, data centre, heavy engineering).

Financial Highlights
* FY26 revenue at ~₹172.79 Cr with 10.29% PAT margins, reflecting stable execution-led profitability.
* Working capital tightly managed at ~90–110 days, largely supported by advance-driven model.
* Higher inventory is execution-linked WIP, aligned with near-term revenue conversion.

Future Outlook
- FY27 guidance strong at ₹260–270 Cr revenue with ~10–11% PAT margins.
- FY28 scaling to ~₹380–385 Cr, supported by planned capacity expansion beyond current limits.
- Long-term vision intact: ₹700+ Cr revenue target by FY30.

Additional Business Insights
* ₹800+ Cr bid pipeline, with management selectively picking high-margin, execution-friendly orders.
* EV exposure rising (~33–40% of automotive), alongside traction in defence and data centre automation.
* Strong competitive positioning with ~20–25% cost advantage vs imports and faster delivery timelines.

Quick Take:
Demand strong, execution sharper, and capacity expansion now the key growth trigger.

reports.text:
🚨Groww founders sell shares worth Rs 250–260 crore, likely to fund startups, philanthropy

Groww’s promoters and promoter group have sold a combined 0.23 percent stake - or 1,44,58,797 shares - worth Rs 250–260 crore over the past few days, marking the founders’ first major liquidity event in nearly a decade.

The current share sale represents less than 1 percent of their stake in the company. 

The proceeds are likely to be deployed toward startup investments and philanthropic initiatives, people aware of the matter told Moneycontrol.

Promoters — including founders Lalit Keshre, Harsh Jain, Ishan Bansal and Neeraj Singh — currently hold a 27.4 percent stake in Groww. This is among the highest promoter shareholdings in new-economy companies.

Groww promoters are active angel investors and have supported several successful startups over the last few years. It is not clear whether the founders will establish a family office or a fund to formalise their investment portfolio.

reports.text:
Deccan Gold Mines Ltd announces a potentially significant Nickel-Copper-PGE discovery in Chhattisgarh 🇮🇳⛏️

The company’s maiden drilling campaign at the Bhalukona project has confirmed extensive Ni-Cu-PGE sulphide mineralisation across a 700m target zone.

Key highlights:
• Over 60m of mineralised gabbro intersected
• Nearly 30m of potentially economic sulphide mineralisation above 0.2% nickel equivalent
• Highest-grade intercept:
2.6m grading:
→ 1.01% Nickel
→ 0.29% Copper
→ 0.2 g/t Palladium
The company has already completed:
• ~1,200 metres of drilling
• Across 7 drill holes
• Over a prospective 1.3 km strike zone
Importantly, microscopic analysis confirmed the presence of:
✅ Pentlandite (Nickel sulphide)
✅ Chalcopyrite (Copper sulphide)
✅ Pyrrhotite sulphides
Management now plans to accelerate drilling to establish a mineable resource and fast-track mining lease applications.
Big picture:
India has been aggressively pushing domestic exploration of critical minerals like Nickel, Copper and PGEs due to rising EV, battery and energy transition demand.

BHARAT FORGE TO SET UP INDIA’S FIRST PRIVATE MARINE GAS TURBINE FACILITY ⚓🚢

• 🇮🇳 Bharat Forge signed MoU with Andhra Pradesh Government to establish India’s first private-sector Marine Gas Turbine (MGT) facility at Visakhapatnam

• 🏭 Facility to come up over ~80 acres within Andhra Pradesh Defence Manufacturing Corridor

• ⚙️ Project to support Indian Navy’s self-reliance through indigenous marine gas turbine repair, overhaul and development capabilities

• 🔧 Phase 1 to focus on Marine GT repair & overhaul including blades, vanes, combustion liners, component manufacturing and testing infrastructure

• 🚢 Facility to be co-located with Naval Dockyard, INS Eksila and Eastern Naval Command HQ at Visakhapatnam

• 🧪 Phase 2 to establish private-sector Marine GT development & assembly hub with full-spectrum testing capability

• 🌍 Company aims to develop and qualify indigenous Marine Gas Turbine systems in India for the first time

• 🤝 Facility also expected to serve as regional MRO hub for friendly foreign navies

• 👷 Project expected to create around 750 direct and indirect employment opportunities

• 🛡️ Initiative aligned with Atmanirbhar Bharat, defence indigenisation and strengthening naval operational readiness

🟢 Impact: Positive (Major strategic entry into defence propulsion systems, strengthens aerospace & naval defence manufacturing capabilities with long-term opportunity potential)

India Renewable Energy Storage – Key Highlights ⚡️
• Renewable energy highly intermittent
• Solar drops after sunset daily
• Wind output fluctuates with weather
• Storage critical for grid stability
• India facing major storage gap
Current Renewable Energy Status
• Renewables form 53% capacity
• Solar crossed 150 GW capacity
• Total installed capacity 532 GW
• Renewable share rapidly increasing
Energy Storage Technologies
• Pumped Hydro Storage (PHS) dominant
• Battery Energy Storage (BESS) growing fast
• Lithium Iron Phosphate batteries preferred
• Storage balances demand-supply mismatch
India Storage Capacity
• PHS operational capacity only 7.2 GW
• BESS installed capacity just 0.27 GW
• PHS target 94 GW by 2035-36
• BESS target 80 GW by 2035-36
Projects Under Development
• 13,120 MW PHS under construction
• 9,580 MW PHS awaiting approval
• 10,658 MW BESS under construction
• 22,347 MW BESS in tender stage
Major Challenges
• Heavy battery import dependence
• China controls 75-80% battery manufacturing
• Geopolitical risks impact costs
• Battery systems still expensive
Global Energy Storage Trends
• China leads global storage market
• China has 66 GW battery capacity
• US follows with 21.8 GW
• Global battery storage accelerating rapidly
Key Concern
• Renewable growth outpacing storage additions
• Grid stability risks increasing
• Night-time power demand remains challenge
• Long-term storage planning insufficient

INDIA’S SMART METERING CYCLE IS ACCELERATING

The Under-Tracked Opportunity
- Genus Power delivered:
  - ₹1,524 Cr quarterly revenue  
  - 63% YoY revenue growth  
  - ₹25,173 Cr order book  
  - 1+ crore meter installations  

- Industry still viewed as:
  - Commodity meter business  
  - Tender-driven opportunity  
  - Low-margin electrical segment  

- Structural reality may differ:
  - India building digital grid infrastructure  

1. Execution Cycle Accelerating
- Q4 growth driven by:
  - Faster AMISP execution  
  - Smart meter rollout ramp-up  
  - Higher deployment activity  

- FY27 guidance indicates:
  - ₹6,000–6,500 Cr revenue target  
  - Strong execution visibility  
  - Multi-year growth runway  

2. Massive Market Opportunity
- India smart meter opportunity:
  - ~31–32 crore meters  

- Only ~15.6 crore tendered:
  - Large untapped market remains  

- Existing scale provides:
  - Utility relationships  
  - Execution credibility  
  - Manufacturing advantages  

3. Scale Becoming Key Advantage
- Annual capacity exceeds:
  - 18 million smart meters  

- Scale supports:
  - Faster deployments  
  - Better procurement economics  
  - Captive AMISP execution  

- Large order book provides:
  - Revenue visibility  
  - Operating leverage potential  

4. Smart Metering Beyond Hardware
- Industry evolving toward:
  - Utility digitisation platforms  
  - Grid intelligence systems  
  - Energy analytics ecosystems  

- Smart meters enable:
  - Real-time billing  
  - Grid optimization  
  - Theft reduction  
  - Load management  

5. Key Watchpoints
- Risks include:
  - DISCOM payment delays  
  - Execution bottlenecks  
  - Raw material inflation  
  - Competitive tendering pressure  

Final Takeaway
- Genus Power may be emerging as a key beneficiary of India’s digital power infrastructure buildout. Strong execution, massive market opportunity and large order visibility suggest India’s smart metering cycle may still be in early stages.  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term Smart Grid Theme

INDIA'S THREE-STAGE NUCLEAR POWER PROGRAMME

• India first uses uranium reactors, then fast breeder reactors, and finally thorium reactors to produce long-term nuclear energy.

• The system is designed to make India self-sufficient in nuclear fuel and electricity generation.

Current Status of Nuclear

• Nuclear power plants are located in different states such as Rajasthan, Gujarat, Maharashtra, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, and Uttar Pradesh.

• Most major plants are built near coastal or water-rich areas because reactors need large amounts of water for cooling.

Gross generation of nuclear power

• Nuclear power generation in India has steadily increased from 2010–11 to 2024–25.

• The graph shows that electricity production from nuclear plants reached its highest level in 2024–25.

KRN heat exchanger Management interview Post Q4 2026 Earnings 

~Margin Expansion: Recent margin growth was driven by a higher share of data center billings (which carry better margins), solid export performance, and slight inventory gains

~FY27 Guidance: Margins are expected to improve by another 1% to 2% (targeting around 19% to 19.5%) in FY27, fueled by government incentives and an expanding export mix.

~Raw Material Inflation: Despite rising copper and aluminum prices, the company mitigates risk by utilizing pass-through price contracts with customers on a QoQ basis

~Capacity Expansion: KRN has expanded its capacity 6 fold compared to its old facility. The company aims for a 50% utilization rate this fiscal year.

~Exports: KRN is witnessing strong demand from North America, the UAE, and Europe. They expect to double their export revenue this year, starting with a record opening order book of around ₹120 crores 

~Sector Demand: While they have 0 revenue from room AC, they are experiencing significant demand in commercial HVAC segments, specifically from data centers and refrigeration 

~Bus & Railway HVAC: After hitting a milestone of supplying 500 bus AC units to OEMs, they are targeting a 15% market share in the bus system market this year. They are currently building a team to begin the 8-to-9-month vendor approval and development process for Indian Railways.

~Government Incentives (PLI & REIPS): KRN has already secured PLI approval, expecting to receive 5% of their finned tube type heat exchanger top line (approx. ₹20–25 crores) this year & They also anticipate Rajasthan's REIPS approval within the next 2 weeks, which will add a 1.5% benefit to their top line.

~KRN currently imports almost 100% of its key materials from Vietnam, Malaysia, Thailand, Korea, and China. They are developing domestic vendors to reduce inventory holding periods, though this shift will take 6 to 8 months

THE OFFSHORE SERVICES CYCLE IN INDIA MAY HAVE JUST ENTERED A NEW PHASE

The Under-Tracked Offshore Opportunity
- India’s largest fleet of multi-support vessels just delivered:
  - Highest-ever annual revenue  
  - Record profitability  
  - Sharp margin expansion  
  - Net cash balance sheet  
  - Expanding offshore fleet  
  - Rising deployment across deepwater projects  

- Yet, offshore oilfield service companies are still often viewed as:
  - Cyclical marine operators  
  - Asset-heavy low-multiple businesses  
  - Commodity-linked service providers  

- The deeper structural story may now be changing:
  - India’s offshore exploration cycle is gradually reviving after years of underinvestment  

1. FY26 Became the Breakout Year
- Consolidated FY26 performance reflected a sharp operating inflection:
  - Revenue crossed ₹1,000 crore  
  - EBITDA surged 83% YoY  
  - PAT nearly tripled YoY  
  - EBITDA margin expanded to 44.7%  

- Strong profitability expansion indicates:
  - Better vessel utilization  
  - Higher-value contract deployment  
  - Improved operating leverage  
  - Better offshore pricing environment  

- Cash profit at ₹411 crore highlights:
  - Strong internal cash generation  
  - Improving balance sheet flexibility  
  - Potential future fleet expansion capability  

- Standalone numbers also confirmed broad-based operational strength rather than one-off gains  

2. Margin Expansion Signals Industry Tightening
- EBITDA margin expansion of nearly 890 bps at consolidated level is significant because offshore service businesses historically operate with:
  - Volatile utilization  
  - High fixed costs  
  - Cyclical charter rates  

- Such margin expansion generally reflects:
  - Better vessel deployment efficiency  
  - Higher charter realizations  
  - Improved contract mix  
  - Stronger offshore demand environment  

- Lump-sum deployment of Seamec III materially supported profitability due to:
  - Better pricing visibility  
  - Improved operating economics  
  - Higher asset productivity  

- If offshore demand remains tight, operating leverage could continue to benefit margins over the medium term  

3. Offshore India Cycle Is Structurally Improving
- India’s offshore oilfield services market is projected to grow strongly through FY31 driven by:
  - Rising deepwater exploration  
  - Offshore reserve replacement needs  
  - Energy security priorities  
  - ONGC capex expansion  

- ONGC securing 7 of 10 blocks under OALP-VIII highlights:
  - Continued offshore exploration focus  
  - Long-duration development pipeline visibility  

- As onshore reserves mature, offshore development becomes increasingly important because:
  - Deepwater fields remain underdeveloped  
  - Domestic production needs structural support  
  - Energy import dependence remains high  

- This could create a multi-year demand cycle for:
  - Diving support vessels  
  - Maintenance support vessels  
  - Offshore installation support  
  - Subsea engineering services  

4. Fleet Expansion & Operational Scale
- Current operational fleet includes:
  - 6 Diving Support Vessels (DSVs)  
  - 1 Offshore Support Vessel (OSV)  
  - 1 Accommodation Barge  
  - 2 Handymax bulk carriers through Dubai subsidiaries  

- Acquisition-led expansion continues through:
  - Seamec Agastya integration  
  - Proposed acquisition of Seamec Anant from HAL Offshore  

- Fleet expansion matters because offshore service businesses are fundamentally:
  - Asset utilization-driven models  
  - Scale-sensitive operations  
  - Contract availability dependent  

- Larger fleets improve:
  - Tender participation capability  
  - Revenue diversification  
  - Geographic flexibility  
  - Customer engagement depth  

5. ONGC Relationship Strengthening
- Multiple operational developments reinforce strategic positioning with ONGC:
  - SEAMEC II contract extended through August 2026  
  - SEAMEC Diamond resumed ONGC operations  
  - Award received for maintenance services of ONGC-owned MSVs

- Long-duration ONGC engagement provides:
  - Revenue visibility  
  - Stable utilization  
  - Lower counterparty risk  
  - Strong domestic positioning  

- Consortium-based maintenance contracts may also deepen:
  - Technical service integration  
  - Long-term operational relevance  
  - Recurring offshore maintenance opportunities  

6. The Global Offshore Linkage
- Client profile includes major offshore and energy players such as:
  - ONGC  
  - ADNOC  
  - Saudi Aramco  
  - L&T Hydrocarbon  
  - POSH  
  - Mermaid Maritime  
  - Kreuz Subsea  

- International exposure matters because offshore cycles are increasingly global and linked to:
  - Crude oil economics  
  - Deepwater capex cycles  
  - Offshore infrastructure spending  
  - Energy security investments  

- Improvement in Dubai subsidiary performance suggests:
  - Better international vessel utilization  
  - Improved regional offshore demand  
  - Recovery in Middle East offshore activity  

7. Balance Sheet Has Turned Into a Strength
- Consolidated balance sheet now reflects:
  - Net cash position  
  - Negative net debt-to-equity  
  - Low leverage metrics  
  - Strong EBITDA coverage  

- This materially changes the financial profile because offshore companies historically faced:
  - High leverage risk  
  - Asset financing pressure  
  - Cyclical balance sheet stress  

- Strong balance sheet flexibility may support:
  - Additional vessel acquisitions  
  - Fleet modernization  
  - Strategic capex opportunities  
  - Better resilience during industry volatility  

8. Key Watch Item – Strait of Hormuz Risk
- Major near-term operational overhang remains:
  - Seamec Paladin stranded post dry dock in Dubai  
  - Awaiting safe navigation through Strait of Hormuz amid West Asia conflict  

- Risks from geopolitical disruption include:
  - Operational delays  
  - Temporary utilization loss  
  - Higher insurance costs  
  - Regional offshore uncertainty  

- However, this currently appears more like:
  - A temporary operational disruption  

- Rather than:
  - Structural demand destruction  

- The broader offshore demand environment remains constructive despite geopolitical volatility  

9. Industry Shift – Cyclical Marine Operator to Strategic Offshore Infrastructure
- Offshore service providers may increasingly evolve from:
  - Pure vessel operators  

- Toward:
  - Strategic offshore infrastructure execution partners  

- Competitive advantage may increasingly depend on:
  - Fleet availability  
  - Technical capabilities  
  - Long-standing PSU relationships  
  - Offshore execution reliability  
  - Multi-region operational capabilities  

- Structural industry tightening combined with rising offshore capex may gradually improve:
  - Asset utilization  
  - Pricing power  
  - Return ratios  
  - Earnings visibility  

Final Takeaway
- SEAMEC’s FY26 performance may indicate more than just a cyclical earnings spike. The broader story could be the early stages of a structural offshore services revival driven by deepwater exploration, ONGC capex acceleration, tightening vessel availability and rising offshore maintenance activity. Record profitability, sharp margin expansion, improving global operations and a net cash balance sheet suggest the company may now be entering a stronger phase of the offshore cycle. While geopolitical disruption around the Strait of Hormuz remains an important near-term watchpoint, the longer-term offshore infrastructure opportunity appears structurally intact.  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term Offshore Energy Services Theme

What is the issue with Jain Resource Recycling results that came yesterday.

Revenue ₹9,543 Cr, up 48%.
PAT ₹352 Cr, up 59%.

Stock fell 17% today.

Let's understand why.

The growth is real:
Copper segment revenue ₹5,262 Cr, up 65%
Copper segment profit ₹194 Cr, up 85%
Lead segment revenue ₹3,818 Cr, up 36%
Lead remains the cash cow, copper is the growth engine

The number that broke the stock:
Net cash from operating activities for FY26 = negative ₹602 Cr.

The company reported ₹352 Cr of profit. The bank account shows a ₹602 Cr outflow from running the business.

Where the cash got stuck:

Trade receivables: ₹129 Cr → ₹476 Cr (+268%)
Inventory: ₹675 Cr → ₹1,477 Cr (+119%)
Both grew far faster than the 48% revenue growth

How the gap was funded:
Short-term borrowings jumped ₹916 Cr → ₹1,271 Cr (+39%)
Growth is now running on debt, not on operating cash

The peer test- Gravita India, same business:
Same scrap recycling- lead, copper, aluminium
FY26 revenue ₹4,265 Cr, PAT ₹378 Cr
Net debt-free after ₹1,000 Cr QIP
Funding new ₹160 Cr copper plant from internal cash
ROIC ~24%

Less revenue. More profit. Zero net debt. Same scrap.

The Key message:

This is not fraud. Copper revenue is genuinely growing 65%, the forward integration into anodes and cathodes is real, and management has been transparent about working capital cycle stretching.
But at 87x earnings and 27x book, the market was pricing perfect execution. The cash flow statement broke that assumption.

One KPI to watch next quarter:
Operating cash flow.
If it turns positive in Q1 FY27, the growth thesis holds.
If it stays negative, today's 17% drop is the first move, not the last.

Why is market so excited about Sakar Healthcare?

It is evolving from a conventional formulation player into a potentially differentiated oncology platform.

What stands out:

* EU-GMP approved oncology manufacturing facility

* Vertically integrated API → formulation → dossier → commercialization model

* 55 oncology molecules developed; 32 launch-ready dossiers

* 125 global filings with 12 marketing authorisations

* Tech transfer relationships with-> Accord, Intas, Torrent, Glenmark, Zydus, Emcure

* Oncology facility still at just ~30% utilisation, leaving room for operating leverage

Q4FY26 numbers indicate an inflection point:

• Revenue: 71 Cr (+42% YoY)
• EBITDA: 26 Cr (+67% YoY)
• PAT: 11 Cr (+91% YoY)
• EBITDA margin: 37% vs 26% in Q3

Importantly, revenue was only +1% QoQ, while EBITDA grew 41% QoQ, implying the quarter was driven by oncology mix improvement + operating leverage, not just topline growth.

Management suggests oncology contribution has scaled from ~21% in FY25 to ~38% in FY26, materially improving profitability given oncology’s structurally superior margin profile.

That said, valuation is no longer cheap.

At ~₹1735 Cr market cap, the stock trades at roughly:
• ~6.8x sales
• ~57x trailing earnings

So this is no longer a value rerating story it is an execution story.

Key risks:
– Approval ≠ commercialization
– Export revenues can be lumpy / tender-driven
– 37% EBITDA margin is unlikely to be the steady-state margin
– If regulated oncology exports don’t scale, valuation can de-rate quickly

Key KPI to track over the next 2–3 quarters: oncology export revenue conversion.

SAI LIFE SCIENCES — EVOLVING INTO A SCIENCE-LED GLOBAL CRDMO PLATFORM 🧬⚗️

- Sai Life Sciences has quietly built:
  - 34 commercial molecules  
  - Relationships with 19 of top 25 global pharma innovators  
  - ₹1,100–1,300 Cr science-led capex pipeline  
  - Integrated Discovery → Development → Commercial manufacturing capabilities  
  - AI-assisted scientific workflows  
  - Expansion into:
    - Peptides  
    - ADCs  
    - Oligonucleotides  

- Yet, market perception still often treats it as just another chemical outsourcing company  

The Real CRDMO Moat
- Advanced CRDMOs are not built only on low-cost manufacturing  
- Real moat comes from:
  - Lifecycle continuity  
  - Embedded pharma relationships  
  - Process familiarity  
  - Regulatory integration  
  - Scientific infrastructure built around molecules over years  

- This fundamentally improves:
  - Customer stickiness  
  - Commercialization economics  
  - Operating leverage  
  - Long-term scalability  

Business Model Transition
- Sai Life Sciences increasingly evolving from:
  - Transactional chemistry vendor  

- Into:
  - Deeply integrated scientific execution platform  

Why This Matters
- Once a molecule progresses through:
  - Discovery  
  - Development  
  - Clinical stages  
  - Commercial manufacturing  

- Switching vendors becomes operationally difficult and risky for pharma innovators  

- This creates:
  - Long-duration customer relationships  
  - Higher visibility revenues  
  - Better margin potential  
  - Stronger utilization stability  

Commercial Molecules = Structural Advantage
- Commercial molecule count matters because:
  - Revenue visibility improves  
  - Manufacturing scale rises  
  - Regulatory familiarity deepens  
  - Incremental margins generally expand  

- Commercial-stage molecules typically carry:
  - Higher stability  
  - Longer lifecycle revenues  
  - Better operating leverage  

Next-Generation Modalities
- Expansion into:
  - Peptides  
  - ADCs  
  - Oligonucleotides  

- Indicates move toward:
  - High-complexity science  
  - Advanced biologics ecosystem  
  - Precision therapeutics manufacturing  

- These segments generally have:
  - Higher entry barriers  
  - Lower competition  
  - Stronger long-term growth potential  

AI & Scientific Infrastructure
- AI-assisted workflows increasingly becoming important in:
  - Molecule optimization  
  - Process development  
  - Data analysis  
  - Scientific productivity  

- Long-term CRDMO differentiation may increasingly depend on:
  - Scientific execution speed  
  - Data infrastructure  
  - Integrated digital workflows  

Capex & Expansion
- ₹1,100–1,300 Cr capex focused on:
  - Scientific infrastructure  
  - Capacity expansion  
  - Advanced modalities  
  - Integrated capabilities  

- Important because CRDMO scaling depends not just on capacity, but on:
  - Regulatory readiness  
  - Technical capabilities  
  - Commercial execution consistency  

Structural Industry Tailwinds
- Global pharma continues outsourcing due to:
  - Cost optimization  
  - Faster development timelines  
  - Complex chemistry requirements  
  - Need for integrated partners  

- India increasingly benefiting from:
  - China+1 diversification  
  - Scientific talent pool  
  - Regulatory track record  
  - Manufacturing scale  

Key Monitorables
- Commercial molecule additions  
- Client concentration  
- Utilization ramp-up  
- Margin expansion trajectory  
- Success in peptides & ADC scale-up  
- Return ratios post capex cycle  
- Regulatory execution consistency  

Final Takeaway
- Sai Life Sciences increasingly appears to be positioning itself as a high-integration scientific CRDMO platform rather than a pure chemical outsourcing vendor. Deep pharma relationships, lifecycle continuity, advanced modality expansion and science-led infrastructure investments could potentially strengthen long-term scalability, customer stickiness and operating leverage over the coming years  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term Structural Story

🏛️ GE Vernova T&D India 

Q4 FY26 Results - Solid 🔥

🔖 Full Year FY26 vs FY25

⬆️ Revenue: 6,206 Cr vs ₹4,292 Cr (+45%)
⬆️ Op. Profit: 1,627 Cr vs ₹831 Cr (+96%)
⬆️ PAT: 1,233 Cr vs ₹608 Cr (+103%)
⬆️ EPS: ₹48.16 vs ₹23.76 (+103%)

🔖 YoY — Q4 FY26 vs Q4 FY25

⬆️ Revenue: 1,637 Cr vs ₹1,153 Cr (+42%)
⬆️ Op. Profit: 445 Cr vs ₹252 Cr (+76%)
⬆️ OPM: 27.2% vs 21.9% (+530 bps)
⬆️ PBT: 469 Cr vs ₹256 Cr (+83%)
⬆️ PAT: 352 Cr vs ₹186 Cr (+89%)
⬆️ EPS: 13.74 vs ₹7.28 (+89%)

🔖 QoQ — Q4 FY26 vs Q3 FY26
⬇️ Revenue: 1,637 Cr vs ₹1,701 Cr (-4%)
⬆️ Op. Profit: 445 Cr vs ₹455 Cr (-2%)
⬆️ OPM: 27.2% vs 26.7% (+45 bps)
⬆️ PBT: 469 Cr vs ₹390 Cr (+20%)
⬆️ PAT: 352 Cr vs ₹291 Cr (+21%)
⬆️ EPS: 13.74 vs ₹11.36 (+21%)

🟢 PAT more than doubled YoY for the full year - FY26 PAT of 1,233 Cr vs 608 Cr in FY25

🟢 OPM at 27.2% - highest Q4 ever, up 530 bps YoY. Structural margin expansion confirmed

🟢 Operating Cash Flow surged to 1,710 Cr for FY26 vs 904 Cr in FY25 - earnings are cash-backed, not just accounting profits

🟢 Cash & equivalents jumped to 1,525 Cr from 471 Cr - fortress balance sheet building

🟢 Exceptional item reversed 5.7 Cr (labour code provision writedown) - clean underlying earnings

🟢 Deloitte issued unmodified audit opinion - no red flags from auditors

🏭 New Capacity Addition - Big Strategic Move

Board approved 55 Cr investment in a brand new facility at Vallam, Tamil Nadu

Products: Disconnectors & Drives for 362 kV Dead Tank Circuit Breakers - currently zero domestic capacity for these products

Entirely new product lines for both India & international export markets - this is not capacity expansion, it’s a greenfield entry into high-voltage equipment segments

First production expected by December 2026 (tentative), fully funded through internal accruals - no equity dilution, no debt. Signals management’s confidence in the multi-year order pipeline and India’s grid modernization mega-theme

⚠️Watch Points

🔴 Revenue dipped 4% QoQ (1,701 Cr → 1,637 Cr) - minor, but seasonal Q4 didn’t outpace Q3 on top line for first time

🔴 Hedge accounting losses of 245 Cr hit Other Comprehensive Income for FY26 - forex derivative drag is significant

So what actually caused the 4% QoQ revenue dip?

It’s simply the nature of project-based revenue recognition - milestone completions and billing cycles don’t follow a straight line quarter to quarter. 

Nothing structurally wrong. The PAT actually grew 21% QoQ despite the lower revenue, which confirms margin discipline held firm. 💪​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​

VODAFONE IDEA LTD Q4FY26 CONCALL HIGHLIGHTS 📡📊

Financial Performance
- Q4 Revenue: ₹11,332 Cr (+2.9% YoY)  
- FY26 Revenue: ₹44,873 Cr (+3.0% YoY)  
- Q4 EBITDA: ₹4,889 Cr (+4.9% YoY)  
- EBITDA Margin improved to 43.1%  
- ARPU increased to ₹190 (+8.3% YoY), marking 19 consecutive quarters of growth  
- FY26 Cash EBITDA stood at ₹9,217 Cr  

Balance Sheet & AGR Relief
- Bank debt reduced to ₹726 Cr vs ₹2,326 Cr YoY  
- DoT finalized AGR dues at ₹64,046 Cr vs earlier ₹87,695 Cr  
- Repayment schedule extended till FY41 with lower payouts till FY35  
- Reported one-time exceptional gain of ₹55,622 Cr  
- Q4 Net Profit surged to ₹51,970 Cr due to accounting adjustment  

Network & Operations
- Subscriber base stabilized at 192.8 million  
- Positive net subscriber additions in February & March  
- Invested nearly ₹16,000 Cr capex over last six quarters  
- Added ~30,000 broadband towers  
- 5G launched in 80+ cities across 17 circles  
- Average data usage increased to 20.2 GB/month  
- Data traffic grew 30%+ YoY  

Enterprise Business
- Strong traction across:
  - BFSI  
  - Manufacturing  
  - Logistics  

- Continued growth in:
  - IoT solutions  
  - Enterprise connectivity  
  - Digital infrastructure services  

Funding & Capex
- Maintaining ₹45,000 Cr three-year capex plan  
- Capex intensity expected to rise from Q1FY27  
- Advanced discussions with SBI-led consortium for:
  - ₹25,000 Cr funded debt  
  - ₹10,000 Cr non-funded facility  

Future Outlook
- Targeting sustained subscriber additions  
- Aiming for double-digit revenue growth  
- Plans to triple EBITDA over next three years  
- Focus areas:
  - Premium customer acquisition  
  - Stronger 4G/5G mix  
  - Brand strengthening post AGR resolution  

Final Takeaway
- Vodafone Idea’s AGR relief materially improves balance sheet visibility and reduces long-term repayment pressure. Stabilizing subscriber trends, rising ARPU, accelerating 5G rollout and planned capex ramp-up indicate gradual operational recovery, though execution and funding remain key monitorables  

Impact
- Positive Long-Term, Execution Dependent

PARK MEDI WORLD Q4FY26 KEY UPDATES 🏥🔥

Massive Capacity Expansion
- Added 610 beds in FY26 — highest ever annual addition  
- Capacity increased from 3,000 to 3,610 beds  
- Current network: 16 hospitals  

Key Additions
- Bathinda: 250 beds  
- Agra: 360 beds  
- Panchkula Greenfield: 350 beds  
- Narela acquisition: 200 beds (Q2FY27 launch)  

FY28 Target
- Targeting 5,460 beds by March 2028  
- ~1,500 beds already under execution  

Occupancy & Patient Growth
- Q4 occupancy: 62.5% vs 59.4%  
- FY26 occupancy: 64.1% (+244 bps)  
- IPD volumes: 95,525 (+18%)  
- OPD volumes: 7.78 lakh (+22%)  
- Total patients served: ~8.7 lakh  

Shift Toward High-End Specialties
- 56.9% revenue now from:
  - Cardiology  
  - Oncology  
  - Neurology  
  - Orthopedics  
  - Gastroenterology  

Key Procedures
- 150+ robotic kidney transplants  
- 4,700 PTCA procedures  
- 1,200 cardiovascular surgeries  
- 2,600 robotic knee & hip replacements  

Oncology Expansion
- Oncology revenue mix increased from 5% to 6.5%  
- Radiation oncology available in 3 hospitals  

Strong Balance Sheet
- Gross debt reduced from ₹450 Cr to ₹28 Cr  
- Cash + FD balance: ₹352 Cr  
- Operating cash flow: ₹329 Cr  
- Net worth doubled to ₹2,022 Cr  

CGHS Rate Hike — Major Tailwind
- Management expects:
  - 5–7.5% revenue benefit over time  

- Full impact likely post Q1FY27  

Receivables Improvement
- Debtor days reduced from 161 to 129 days  
- Collection efficiency improved significantly  

UP Expansion Strategy
- Uttar Pradesh identified as major growth market  
- Targeting 1,060+ beds in UP by FY28  

Competitive Advantages
- Low capex per bed (~₹34–35 lakh)  
- Affordable ARPOB model  
- Cluster-based expansion strategy  
- Strong doctor retention  
- High occupancy-led operating leverage  

Final Takeaway
- Park Medi World is aggressively scaling its North India healthcare platform through capacity expansion, specialty mix improvement and balance sheet strengthening. Strong cash flows, lower debt and improving occupancy provide strong visibility for multi-year growth ahead

The Illusion of Plenty: Oil’s Floor is Thinning

Global inventories are in a freefall, with 280 million barrels already consumed to buffer the current conflict. Despite a headline 8.4 billion barrels, only 0.8 billion are realistically accessible.

The "Strait of Hormuz" closure is a systemic shock. OECD stocks could hit operational stress levels by June, falling toward a 6.8 billion barrel floor by September if demand destruction fails to scale.

Bottom Line: Inventory is the only shock absorber left, and it's running dry. Expect extreme price volatility as the market shifts from managed SPR releases to a forced, high-price rationing regime.

**INDIA’S ELECTRICITY DISTRIBUTION CAPEX CYCLE MAY HAVE TURNED UP AGAIN ⚡️📈**

- Electricity distribution capex cycle showed strong revival signs in FY26 after nearly four years of contraction  
- Key indicators surged:
  - New project announcements ▲ 8.4x YoY  
  - Projects under implementation ▲ 4.6x YoY  
  - Outstanding project pipeline ▲ 3.5x YoY  

- Previous cycle peaked during FY16–FY20:
  - FY16 announcements reached ₹13,010 Cr across 13 projects  
  - Outstanding pipeline crossed ₹2 lakh Cr  
  - Projects under implementation peaked at 76 worth ₹13,330 Cr in FY20  

- By FY25, the cycle had almost collapsed:
  - Only 3 announcements worth ₹64 Cr  
  - 5 projects under implementation worth ₹144 Cr  
  - Outstanding pipeline reduced to 7 projects worth ₹197 Cr  
  - Project completions were zero in FY25  

- FY26 marked clear reversal with project completions resuming:
  - 3 completions vs 0 in FY25  

- Possible drivers behind the revival:
  - RDSS rollout acceleration  
  - Discom restructuring  
  - Renewable energy integration needs  
  - T&D infrastructure expansion  
  - Rising electricity demand  

- Distribution infrastructure historically remained weakest link in India’s power ecosystem due to:
  - High AT&C losses  
  - Weak discom finances  
  - Underinvestment in grid infrastructure  

- Sustained revival could benefit:
  - Power distribution EPC companies  
  - Cable & conductor manufacturers  
  - Transformer makers  
  - Smart metering ecosystem  
  - Grid automation players  
  - T&D infrastructure companies  

- Simultaneous increase in:
  - Project count  
  - Project value  
  - Execution activity  

strongly suggests a new capex cycle may be forming in India’s power distribution sector  

**Final Takeaway**
- India’s electricity distribution segment appears to be entering a fresh investment cycle driven by grid modernization, renewable integration and discom reforms. If execution sustains, distribution infrastructure could become one of the next major long-duration themes within the broader Indian power capex story  

**Impact**
- Positive Long-Term Structural Theme

Already taken 9-12% price hike in Q4 post which there has been double-digit inflation in cost and further price hike to follow

Jefferies On Kaynes

Target Price ₹3970

Earlier Target ₹4515

Recommendation Buy

From Strength to Strain

Post 55% dip from Oct25-peak, KAYNES now trades at 43x FY27 PE, much below its hist trading avg of 65x

Cut FY27-28 EPS by mid-teens, and estimate working capital to stay high at >100days

In FY26-29e, we est +43% EPS CAGR on low base, as OSAT+PCB sales can accrue from FY27

JPMorgan On Siemens Energy

Target Price ₹2600

Recommendation Neutral

Headline print in-line, but with softer 'transmission' numbers

EBIT margins expanded 140bps year-on-year, showing operating leverage is continuing to play out

'Generation' segment revenue growth and margins were comfortably ahead of expectations

Target Price ₹5954

Recommendation Buy

Q4FY26: Beat on operating performance

Robust ordering in FY26

Balance sheet improvement continues
